original_hypothesis.title: Owner Changing transferAllowedContract Breaks All MergeTgt Claim Operations on Arbitrum
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: On Arbitrum (chainId 42161), MergeTgt is set as the transferAllowedContract on Titn. When MergeTgt calls titn.safeTransfer in claimTitn or withdrawRemainingTitn, the _validateTransfer function allows it because from == transferAllowedContract. If the owner changes transferAllowedContract to a different address (e.g., a staking contract), MergeTgt loses its exemption. All safeTransfer calls from MergeTgt will revert, permanently DoS-ing all TITN claims.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: Titn.sol allows only ONE transferAllowedContract at a time. The deployment sets this to MergeTgt. If owner changes it, there's no multi-address allowlist — MergeTgt is blocked. Since block.chainid == 42161 restricts ALL Arbitrum transfers, MergeTgt has no other bypass.
deployment_assumptions: Owner does not change transferAllowedContract away from MergeTgt while claims are pending
original_hypothesis.config_evidence: arbitrumSetup.ts line: await titn.setTransferAllowedContract(deploymentMergeTgt.address) — sets MergeTgt as the single allowed contract

proof_of_signal.pattern: Single transferAllowedContract address; _validateTransfer checks from != transferAllowedContract

preconditions: Owner calls setTransferAllowedContract with address != MergeTgt
Users still have pending TITN claims in MergeTgt

initial_code_evidence.vulnerable_pattern: Only one transferAllowedContract at a time; changing it removes MergeTgt exemption
initial_code_evidence.specific_concern: MergeTgt.claimTitn and withdrawRemainingTitn call titn.safeTransfer which triggers _validateTransfer
initial_code_evidence.missing_safeguards: No multi-address allowlist; no protection preventing change while claims are pending

analysis_questions: Would the owner have a legitimate reason to change transferAllowedContract during merge period?
Is there a staking contract that needs to be the transferAllowedContract simultaneously?
Could an address allowlist replace the single-address pattern?
validation_requirements: Set transferAllowedContract to a non-MergeTgt address
Attempt claimTitn — verify it reverts with BridgedTokensTransferLocked
Verify no workaround exists on Arbitrum

original_hypothesis.preliminary_attack_scenario: Owner sets transferAllowedContract to a staking contract address. MergeTgt loses its transfer exemption on Arbitrum. Users calling claimTitn or withdrawRemainingTitn trigger titn.safeTransfer which reverts because MergeTgt is no longer the transferAllowedContract and block.chainid == 42161 restricts all transfers.

potential_impact_if_confirmed.financial_impact: All pending TITN claims become inaccessible
potential_impact_if_confirmed.affected_users: All users with unclaimed TITN in MergeTgt
potential_impact_if_confirmed.recovery_possibility: Owner can restore by setting transferAllowedContract back to MergeTgt

original_hypothesis.test_coverage_gap: No test for interaction between transferAllowedContract changes and MergeTgt operations

validation_result.impact_summary: Filtered before validation: attack_realism is LOW
validation_result.detailed_analysis: This hypothesis was auto-rejected because its attack_realism grade is LOW. Preconditions require actions outside the attacker's control (off-chain compromise, victim cooperation, or admin collusion). No LLM validation was performed.
